stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is text generation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformer, Llama, and AI/ML communities.

stories.title: SpaceX just filed for what could be the biggest IPO ever
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: SpaceX has officially filed its S-1 prospectus with the SEC, initiating the IPO process.
The potential listing on the Nasdaq under the ticker SPCX could be one of the largest IPOs in history.
The company reported significant revenue, generating $18.67 billion in 2025.

stories.title: Google publishes exploit code threatening millions of Chromium users
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Google disclosed exploit code affecting millions of Chromium users.
The vulnerability was reported nearly 29 months prior to the public disclosure.
The exploit code was published before the corresponding patch was made available.

stories.title: GitHub confirms breach of 3,800 repos via malicious VSCode extension
stories.author: Timofeibu
bullet_points: GitHub confirmed a security breach affecting approximately 3,800 repositories.
The vector for the breach was identified as a malicious VSCode extension.
This highlights significant supply chain and dependency risks within developer tooling.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function in language modeling.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, suggesting it is a modern, accessible LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for enhanced performance on Apple Silicon.
This integration leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, for optimization.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling ongoing hardware/software optimization efforts.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device for privacy.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed in partnership with ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: You can now remix other people’s YouTube Shorts with AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Google has introduced a new 'YouTube Shorts Remix' feature powered by Gemini Omni.
This feature allows users to restyle existing YouTube Shorts or insert themselves into other creators' videos.
Users can prompt Gemini to 'reimagine' a Short, enabling AI-driven video modification.

stories.title: Famously secret about its finances, SpaceX opens its books for the first time
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX is making a rare move by opening up its financial records to the public.
The company claims to have identified the 'largest TAM in human history,' signaling massive future growth potential.
This move represents a significant shift in transparency for a notoriously secretive aerospace/tech giant.

stories.title: ACL-Verbatim: hallucination-free question answering for research
stories.author: Ádám Kovács
bullet_points: Introduces ACL-Verbatim, a system designed to eliminate LLM hallucinations in academic research.
The method uses an extractive question answering system (VerbatimRAG) to map queries directly to verbatim text spans in source documents.
Addresses a critical need for reliable, factually accurate information extraction from trusted sources like the ACL Anthology.

stories.title: AutoRPA: Efficient GUI Automation through LLM-Driven Code Synthesis from Interactions
stories.author: Yufei Yin
bullet_points: Introduces AutoRPA, a method to improve GUI automation efficiency using LLM-driven code synthesis.
Addresses the inefficiency of repeatedly invoking LLM reasoning (like ReAct) for repetitive, multi-step GUI tasks.
Aims to bridge the gap between the high manual effort of traditional RPA and the computational overhead of pure LLM agents.

stories.title: TIDE: Efficient and Lossless MoE Diffusion LLM Inference with I/O-aware Expert Offload
stories.author: Yuzhang Shang
bullet_points: Introduces TIDE, a method for efficient and lossless inference of MoE Diffusion LLMs (dLLMs).
Addresses the deployment challenge of large-scale dLLMs on resource-constrained devices.
Mitigates existing bottlenecks in dLLM inference, specifically focusing on I/O overhead and compute limitations.

stories.title: ClinSeekAgent: Automating Multimodal Evidence Seeking for Agentic Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Yuyin Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces ClinSeekAgent, an automated agentic framework designed for dynamic multimodal evidence seeking in clinical settings.
Addresses a key limitation in current LLM applications: moving beyond passive consumption of curated data to active, iterative evidence gathering.
Focuses on enabling agentic systems to synthesize evidence from heterogeneous, real-world clinical sources for improved decision support.

stories.title: ThoughtTrace: Understanding User Thoughts in Real-World LLM Interactions
stories.author: Tianmin Shu
bullet_points: Introduces ThoughtTrace, a novel, large-scale dataset designed to capture users' internal thoughts during LLM interactions, moving beyond just transcribed dialogue.
The dataset pairs real-world multi-turn conversations with self-reported user motivations (reasons for prompts) and reactions to AI responses.
ThoughtTrace was collected across 20 different language models, providing a broad and valuable resource for future AI research.

stories.title: GitHub Got Breached Through a VS Code Extension. MCP Servers Are Next.
stories.author: niclydon
bullet_points: GitHub experienced a security breach involving an employee device.
The compromise was linked to a vulnerability exploited via a VS Code extension.
The incident raises concerns about the security of MCP Servers.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Samsung has a tentative deal with workers to avoid a memory chip strike
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Samsung avoided a potential 18-day strike involving over 47,000 workers.
The dispute centered on collapsed bonus payment negotiations between the company and its union.
The averted strike was significant given ongoing global constraints in memory chip production.

stories.title: Leaving the V8 in the past: The all-electric Mercedes-AMG GT 4-Door
stories.author: Michael Teo Van Runkle
bullet_points: Mercedes-AMG is transitioning its high-performance GT line to an all-electric powertrain.
The electric version demonstrates impressive performance metrics, including rapid acceleration (0-60 time).
The vehicle shows strong energy efficiency, highlighted by its high miles/kWh rating.

stories.title: Chickens without eggs? De-extinction company creates artificial egg.
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: De-extinction company Colossal has developed a method to create artificial eggs.
This development is seen as a potentially useful tool for the field of developmental biology.
The story touches upon advanced genetic and biological engineering techniques.

stories.title: Automated ICD Classification of Psychiatric Diagnoses: From Classical NLP to Large Language Models
stories.author: Enrique Baca-García
bullet_points: The research addresses the administrative burden of coding psychiatric diagnoses by automating the mapping of free-text descriptions to ICD codes.
It compares various NLP and ML techniques, ranging from classical methods to Large Language Models (LLMs), for this classification task.
The study utilized a substantial dataset of 145,513 Spanish psychiatric descriptions for evaluation.

stories.title: Detecting Trojaned DNNs via Spectral Regression Analysis
stories.author: Paolo Tonella
bullet_points: Addresses the security risk of Trojan attacks in Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) during the fine-tuning process.
Introduces MIST, a new detection method that analyzes changes in a model's internal representations during fine-tuning.
MIST characterizes benign model evolution using pre-activation spectra instead of trying to reconstruct specific trigger conditions.

stories.title: Decoupling Communication from Policy: Robust MARL under Bandwidth Constraints
stories.author: Sonia Vanier
bullet_points: Addresses a critical challenge in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL): performance degradation under severe bandwidth constraints.
Identifies the problem of coupled bottlenecks where shared latent representations are used for both policy and communication.
Proposes decoupling communication from policy to maintain robust performance despite limited message size, relevant for applications like drone swarms.

stories.title: Fine-grained Claim-level RAG Benchmark for Law
stories.author: Domenico Bianculli
bullet_points: The paper introduces a fine-grained Claim-level RAG Benchmark specifically tailored for the legal domain.
It addresses the persistent issue of hallucinations in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems, even in high-stakes legal applications.
The work emphasizes the necessity of detailed, fine-grained evaluation methods to improve the reliability of LLM-based QA in specialized fields.

stories.title: Grounding Driving VLA via Inverse Kinematics
stories.author: Hyunjung Shim
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current Driving Vision-Language Agents (VLAs) that ignore visual tokens during trajectory prediction.
The authors argue that trajectory recovery is structurally ill-posed because existing VLAs only provide a current visual state, not a future one, which is needed for inverse kinematics.
The proposed solution involves redesigning the task formulation to incorporate both current and future visual states as boundary conditions.

stories.title: Multi-axis Analysis of Image Manipulation Localization
stories.author: Bryan A. Plummer
bullet_points: The proliferation of generative AI makes creating convincing image manipulations increasingly easy.
Manipulated images pose a growing threat by enabling misinformation and influencing public opinion.
The research addresses a gap in detecting advanced image manipulations across various visual domains.

stories.title: HaorFloodAlert: Deseasonalized ML Ensemble for 72-Hour Flood Prediction in Bangladesh Haor Wetlands
stories.author: Md. Zakir Hossen
bullet_points: Introduces HaorFloodAlert, a novel ML ensemble designed specifically for predicting flash floods in Bangladesh's Haor wetlands.
Addresses a critical gap in current flood prediction models, which are often designed for riverine systems and fail to capture backwater dynamics in flat basins.
The model incorporates deseasonalization techniques to overcome the confounding effect of temperature on flood probability forecasting.

stories.title: Interpretable Computer Vision for Defect Detection in X-ray Tomography of Aerospace SiC/SiC Composites
stories.author: Thomas Philippe
bullet_points: Addresses the 'black-box' problem in deep learning applied to industrial inspection.
Focuses on using interpretable computer vision for defect detection in aerospace SiC/SiC composites via X-ray CT.
Introduces p-ResNet-50, a novel convolutional framework designed to provide transparency in automated quality control.

stories.title: SAGE: Scalable Automatic Gating Ensemble for Confident Negative Harvesting in Fraud Detection
stories.author: Amit Goyal
bullet_points: Addresses music streaming fraud, where artificial stream inflation manipulates rankings and royalties.
Introduces SAGE, a novel counterfactual-aware negative harvesting ensemble for detection.
Tackles the challenge of distinguishing sophisticated fraud patterns from legitimate user behavior (e.g., super-fans).

stories.title: When Does Model Collapse Occur in Structured Interactive Learning?
stories.author: Weijie Su
bullet_points: The rise of generative AI creates interactive learning environments where models are trained on synthetic data from other models.
This paradigm challenges classical statistical learning by meaning training data is no longer purely drawn from the target population.
The research addresses the emerging issue of 'model collapse' within these continuously updated, interactive learning systems.

stories.title: From Seeing to Thinking: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning Improves Post-Training of Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Yuyin Zhou
bullet_points: The study addresses a limitation in Vision-Language Models (VLMs), suggesting visual perception, not reasoning, is the primary bottleneck in visual tasks.
The research proposes a novel training methodology by decoupling VLM capabilities into three distinct stages: visual perception, visual reasoning, and textual reasoning.
This decomposition aims to improve the post-training performance of VLMs by specializing training for each core capability.

stories.title: KoRe: Compact Knowledge Representations for Large Language Models
stories.author: Jacopo Staiano
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inherent flaw in LLMs encoding world knowledge solely within their opaque parameters.
It proposes 'KoRe,' a method for creating compact knowledge representations to supplement LLMs.
The research contrasts LLM parameter-based knowledge with the human-readable structure of Knowledge Graphs.

stories.title: Rethinking Visual Attribution for Chest X-ray Reasoning in Large Vision Language Models
stories.author: Aidong Zhang
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of visual grounding and trustworthiness in Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) used for medical imaging (Chest X-rays).
Highlights the gap in current research: existing visual attribution methods are often unverified regarding whether they truly reflect the model's visual reasoning.
The research tackles the challenge of validating internal model reasoning when ground-truth annotations are unavailable.

stories.title: MixRea: Benchmarking Explicit-Implicit Reasoning in Large Language Models
stories.author: Yanru Zhang
bullet_points: The research introduces 'MixRea' to benchmark explicit-implicit reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs).
It investigates whether LLMs suffer from a form of 'inattentional blindness,' failing to notice subtle cues despite explicit instructions.
This work is relevant as LLMs are increasingly used in high-stakes decision-making processes.

stories.title: BalanceRAG: Joint Risk Calibration for Cascaded Retrieval-Augmented Generation
stories.author: Zhiyuan Wang
bullet_points: Introduces 'BalanceRAG,' a novel approach to improve LLM factuality by using a cascaded Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system.
The system prioritizes an LLM-only answer first, escalating to RAG only when the primary model expresses uncertainty.
It includes a mechanism for the model to abstain from answering when neither the LLM nor RAG branch is sufficiently trustworthy.

stories.title: CopT: Contrastive On-Policy Thinking with Continuous Spaces for General and Agentic Reasoning
stories.author: Wenke Lee
bullet_points: Introduces CopT, a new reasoning pipeline designed to improve upon standard Chain-of-Thought (CoT) methods in LLMs.
Addresses the inefficiency of CoT by allowing for 'performative reasoning'—providing answers before extended thinking is complete.
CopT utilizes a contrastive, on-policy approach with continuous spaces to enhance general and agentic reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Chromium publishes fixed exploit 4 years later, turns out it's actually unfixed
stories.author: infosec.exchange via hugoarnal
bullet_points: Chromium released a security patch for an exploit that was reportedly fixed years ago.
The analysis suggests the published fix may be ineffective or incomplete.
The story highlights potential issues in the vulnerability disclosure and patching lifecycle of major software projects.

stories.title: GitHub Source Code Breach - TeamPCP Claims Access to Internal Source Code
stories.author: cybersecuritynews.com via mseri
bullet_points: A security incident involving a claimed breach of GitHub's internal source code.
The entity making the claim is identified as TeamPCP.
The story originates from the Lobsters forum, suggesting community discussion around the event.

stories.title: XSS Is Deadly for Passkeys: The Hidden Risk of Attestation None
stories.author: scotthelme.co.uk via fazalmajid
bullet_points: The article discusses a critical security vulnerability related to XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) affecting Passkeys.
The core issue revolves around the risk associated with 'Attestation None' in the context of passkey implementation.
This highlights a niche but important security concern within modern authentication protocols.

stories.title: Choosing the Right RAG Strategy A Complete Decision Guide to Chunking, Agentic RAG, and GraphRAG
stories.author: sreeni5018
bullet_points: The article provides a comprehensive guide on selecting appropriate Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) strategies.
It covers key technical components such as chunking strategies, Agentic RAG, and GraphRAG.
It targets RAG builders by addressing common pipeline challenges and decision-making processes.

stories.title: I built a Claude Code skill that scores your legacy Java code 1–100 and modernizes it to Java 21
stories.author: santosh_ravitejagoteti_
bullet_points: The article details the creation of a Claude Code skill designed for Java modernization.
The tool assesses legacy Java code (e.g., Java 8) and provides a modernization score (1-100).
It specifically targets the challenges associated with migrating older Java codebases to modern versions like Java 21.
